success_surface: Multi-row deletion task with pattern-based filtering and index-safe execution
effective_behaviors: Performed comprehensive data inspection before modification to identify all target rows
Collected all rows to be deleted into a list before executing any deletions
Deleted rows in reverse order (bottom to top) to prevent index shifting errors
Used openpyxl for row deletion operations requiring structure preservation
Programmatic sheet name discovery before accessing specific worksheets
Verified post-operation state to confirm target rows were removed
Saved to output path rather than modifying source file in-place
evidence: Agent first inspected full Sheet1 data using pandas to identify exact rows matching Z1/X1 pattern
rows_to_delete list was populated with all matching row indices before any ws.delete_rows() call
Delete loop used reversed(rows_to_delete) ensuring higher indices processed first
After deletion, verification printed remaining row count and checked that no Z1/X1 values remained
output_path was used for wb.save() preserving original workbook untouched
Available sheets listed programmatically via pd.ExcelFile.sheet_names

memory_items.title: Index-Safe Multi-Row Deletion
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple rows, collect all target row indices first, then delete in reverse order (highest to lowest)
memory_items.generalization: This prevents index shifting that causes skipped rows or incorrect deletions when removing consecutive or scattered rows from a worksheet.

memory_items.title: Inspect-Then-Execute Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Always examine complete dataset and identify all target items before applying any modifications
memory_items.generalization: Ensures accurate scope determination and prevents partial edits or unintended consequences from premature modifications.

memory_items.title: Post-Operation Verification
memory_items.lesson: Verify output state matches expectations after structural changes like row deletion
memory_items.generalization: Check row counts, re-scan for target patterns, and confirm expected content remains while targets are removed.

memory_items.title: Tool Selection for Row Operations
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl for structural operations requiring row/column deletion and style preservation
memory_items.generalization: While pandas handles data filtering well, openpyxl provides native delete_rows() methods that maintain Excel structure integrity.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Index-Safe Deletion**: When deleting multiple rows, collect all target indices first, then delete in reverse order (highest to lowest). This prevents index shifting that skips rows or causes incorrect deletions.
- **Inspect-Then-Execute**: Examine the complete dataset and identify all target items before applying modifications. Ensure accurate scope determination.
- **Post-Operation Verification**: After structural changes, verify output state by checking row counts and re-scanning to confirm targets are removed while expected content remains.
- **Tool Selection for Structural Ops**: Use openpyxl's delete_rows() for row deletion operations requiring structure preservation; pandas alone cannot maintain Excel layout during multi-row removal.
